CONTENT.Honours: Mexico U23CONCACAF Olympic Qualifying Championship: 2020IndividualLiga MX Best XI: Clausura 2018CONCACAF Champions League Best XI: 2022
CONTENT.Club career: Mozo made his Liga MX debut on 16 September 2017, in a match that ended as a 1–1 tie against Guadalajara. At the end of the Clausura 2018, he was listed on the Best XI of the tournament.In May 2022, Mozo joined C.D. Guadalajara.
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 19 May 2024As of match played 16 October 2019
CONTENT.International career: In May 2019, Mozo was called up by Jaime Lozano to participate in that year's Toulon Tournament. In the first group stage match against Bahrain, he would win the Player of the match award. Playing all matches of the tournament, Mexico would place third in the competition.Mozo also participated at the 2020 CONCACAF Olympic Qualifying Championship, where Mexico won the competition.In September 2019, Mozo received his first senior national team call-up by Gerardo Martino for a set of friendly matches and on 2 October, he made his debut against Trinidad and Tobago.

SUMMARY: Alan Mozo Rodríguez (born 5 April 1997) is a Mexican professional footballer who plays as a right-back for Liga MX club Guadalajara.
